name: Sogn og Fjordane (Norway, 1983–2019)
description: A white field charged with three blue elongated triangles arranged in horizontal pale, points toward the fly.
history: Granted to Sogn og Fjordane county on 6 May 1983. The three triangles represent the three traditional districts. The county was abolished on 1 January 2020 when it was merged with Hordaland into the new Vestland county.

features.symbolism: three blue triangular figures on a silver (white) field, representing the three traditional districts of the county: Sogn, Sunnfjord, and Nordfjord; the points refer to the steep peaks and fjords characteristic of the region
the three districts of the county and the mountain peaks / fjords characteristic of western Norway
